name: Baked Apple Dumplings with Cranberry Filling
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. In a bowl, mix together sour cream, brown sugar, dried cranberries, and grated orange peel.
3. Core the apples, making a hole with a diameter of 1-1/4 inches. Do not cut through the bottom or peel the apples.
4. Divide the pastry into 6 equal pieces. Roll out one piece on a well-floured surface to form a 9-inch square.
5. Brush the dough lightly with water and place an apple in the center.
6. Fill the apple with the sour cream mixture, then fold and pinch the edges to enclose the fruit securely.
7. Trim the excess pastry at the top, following the curve of the apple, and reserve the trimmed pieces.
8. Press the seams against the apple. Refrigerate any remaining apples while preparing the rest.
9. Brush each dumpling with milk and sprinkle with 1 teaspoon granulated sugar.
10. Place the dumplings on a baking sheet, about 2 inches apart.
11. Prick the dough several times with a fork, being careful not to pierce the apple skin.
12. Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes or until the apples are tender and the pastry is golden brown.
13. Serve warm.
